texto: Nothing is ever lost by courtesy. It is the cheapest of pleasures costs nothing and conveys much. It pleases him who gives and receives and thus like mercy is twice blessed.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Erastus Wiman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Nothing is ever lost by courtesy. It is the cheapest of pleasures costs nothing and conveys much.…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, amizade, nothing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
